mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances when the trigger activates over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust demand without overheating, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and stable CPIAUCSL yields negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of cases over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production above its 12-month moving average reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO crossing above its 12-month average fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a rank IC greater than 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate wage income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth correlates with negative 20-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of observations across the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0 percent, borrowing costs for corporations and households rise sufficiently to suppress capital expenditure and equity risk appetite, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of periods when FEDFUNDS remains above 2.0 percent over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and real gross domestic product signal synchronized strength in residential investment and broad economic output, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent indicates a sudden contraction in factory output and supply chain bottlenecks, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances following the trigger activation over a 2-year rolling window.
